=== info.txt
===================
===================
===================
Bob Dylan
Frankenhalle
Nuremberg, Germany
24 April 2002

I have no info for this recording. The sound is really good.
"It's All Over Now, Baby Blue" is cut at the start and start abruptly during the harmonica solo. A little cut at the start of Cd2
Drop between Cdr.


1 intro + Hallelujah, I'm Ready To Go (acoustic) (Larry on mandolin), 2 I'll Remember You (acoustic) (Larry on cittern), 3 It's Alright, Ma (I'm Only Bleeding) (acoustic) (Larry on cittern and Charlie on dobro), 4 It's All Over Now, Baby Blue (acoustic), 5 Tweedle Dee & Tweedle Dum, 6 Can't Wait, 7 Subterranean Homesick Blues (Larry on slide guitar), 8 Moonlight (Bob on harp and Tony on standup bass), 9 Tangled Up In Blue (acoustic), 10 Boots Of Spanish Leather (acoustic), 11 Masters Of War (acoustic),

CD2

1 Summer Days (Tony on standup bass), 2 Standing In The Doorway, 3 The Wicked Messenger (Bob on harp), 4 Leopard-Skin Pill-Box Hat, 5 Country Pie, 6 Like A Rolling Stone, 7 I Shall Be Released (acoustic) (Bob on harp), 8 Honest With Me (Larry on slide guitar), 9 Blowin' In The Wind (acoustic) (Bob on harp), 10 All Along The Watchtower


Concert # 1416 of The Never-Ending Tour. Concert # 15 of the 2002 Europe Spring Tour. 2002 concert # 33.

Second concert with the 14th Never-Ending Tour Band: Bob Dylan (vocal & guitar), Charlie Sexton (guitar), Larry Campbell (guitar, mandolin, pedal steel guitar & electric slide guitar), Tony Garnier (bass), Jim Keltner (drums & percussion).

1–4, 9-11, 18, 20 acoustic with the band.
4, 8, 14, 18, 20 Bob Dylan (harmonica).
2, 3 Larry Campbell (cittern).
1, 18, 20 Larry Campbell & Charlie Sexton (backup vocals).

Note. First acoustic version of I'll Remember You!
17 new songs (80%) compared to previous concert. 1 new song for this tour
